summary: This cluster of research focuses on the ecological dynamics of freshwater algal communities in the Togyo Reservoir in Chulwon, Korea. The studies investigated the composition and relative abundance of phytoplankton and periphyton, identifying 150 phytoplankton taxa with a notable dominance of diatoms over green algae. A key finding was the significant increase in epipelic algae, particularly pennate diatoms, whose prevalence was linked to the presence of raphe structures facilitating movement. The geographic scope was strictly limited to this single Korean reservoir, with the research emphasizing the structural patterns of periphyton and phytoplankton distribution within that specific ecosystem.
